mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings contraction, triggering immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC, or forward returns turn negative when both series exceed their 80th percentile historical thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms an overheating economy that supports earnings revisions and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon specifically during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward returns become negative when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% during periods where the 10-year Treasury yield curve inverts, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 0.25% per annum initiates a tightening monetary regime that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, systematically generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of observations post-2015, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and rising real consumer credit balances reflect synchronized labor market strength and consumer spending momentum, which mechanically lifts equity demand and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random when either proxy falls below its 24-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects rising capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which directly correlates with higher corporate output and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of the macro regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% annually triggers a regime shift where real purchasing power erosion and anticipated policy tightening compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation spike, or the signal shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns in walk-forward tests.
